prompt: Write a 150-word short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers something unexpected in the fog. Do not use dialogue.
response:
The lighthouse keeper adjusted his coat against the damp chill as he climbed the spiral stairs. Outside, the fog clung thick and silent, swallowing the beam of light whole.  

At the base of the tower, something glinted in the mist. He hesitated, then stepped closer. A small boat had washed ashore, its hull cracked but intact. Inside lay a wooden chest, its lid slightly ajar.  

His pulse quickened as he pried it open. Nestled inside was not gold or jewels, but a collection of letters—hundreds of them—each addressed in delicate handwriting to someone long gone. The dates spanned decades, the ink faded but the words still tender.  

The keeper sat back, staring at the sea. Who had sent them? Why had they never been delivered? The fog curled around him, heavy with secrets. That night, he read every one by lamplight, the lighthouse beam turning, unanswered, into the dark.
